title: CI: emergency fix for broken go get
body:
go get is deprecated, we should use go install instead.

Also for some reason go get -u golang.org/x/tools/cmd/goimports is
broken at the moment, thus failing CI jobs where we have to install
this. Switching to go install seems to fix it.


<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note

For more information on release notes please follow the kubernetes model:

```release-note

```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Made it past `build 35` which is more than any other attempt has managed. Thank you.

/lgtm
/hold

**IMPORTANT**: I expect that go install will also fail in the near future. The difference is that go install will use the latest tag while go get uses main/master AFAICT.

The problem is that go 1.18 removes the old `// +build` build tag syntax support. Therefore new code can remove this tag because they only target go v1.17 and v1.18. go v1.16 (shipped in fedora 35) is now end of life and thus no longer supported by upstream code. So the root problem is that we (fedora 35) use an unsupported go version.

If we have to continue to use fedora35 with go 1.16 I expect us to run into more problems. This is not limited to go get/go install, it will also start affecting the actual builds.

Thanks for the heads-up. Is this something that could be fixed by using per-branch tags instead of `latest`?
```
GOIMPORTS_REVISION=latest
if some-old-version then GOIMPORTS_REVISION=x.y
...
 		$(call go-install,golang.org/x/tools/cmd/goimports ${GOIMPORTS_REVISION}); \
```

LGTM
We have doc on the Buildah install guide (https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/install.md#vendoring) does that need to change too and/or the Buildah Makefile?  Maybe other projects?

@TomSweeneyRedHat sorry my commit message is wrong, `go get -u` to install binaries is deprecated in favour of go install.
Using go get for module management is correct, so no changes required.

@Luap99: only [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) org members may request cherry picks. You can still do the cherry-pick manually.
